businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker pivoting to a 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomous driving, robotics, energy, and AI chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla is transitioning from EV manufacturing to an integrated AI, robotics, and energy platform. FY2025 revenue $94.8B (-2.9% YoY), net income $3.79B (-46.5% YoY). Friday close $391.20 (-0.96%), premarket $400.88 (+2.47%). Terafab AI chip fab confirmed for 3/21 (Saturday) launch. Model S/X production ended, Fremont factory converting to Optimus robot production. BYD overtook Tesla globally with 2.26M EV deliveries (+28%) vs Tesla's 1.64M (-8%), unveiling 11 new models including a 640-mile sedan and 5-minute fast charging.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy storage and generation (Megapack, Powerwall, Solar)
Services and charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA upgrades)
keyProducts: Terafab - AI chip manufacturing facility (3/21 launch)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extremely elevated valuation reflecting AI, robotics, and energy platform premium

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Reflects FY2026 improvement expectations, still highest in sector

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (YoY)
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025 contraction from delivery decline and price cuts

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Margin compression from price war with BYD

keyMetrics.name: FCF
keyMetrics.interpretation: Healthy cash generation. $44B total cash on hand

keyMetrics.name: Debt-to-Equity
keyMetrics.interpretation: Excellent balance sheet. Among lowest in big tech

keyMetrics.name: ROE
keyMetrics.interpretation: Low vs big tech peers. Margin recovery is critical

keyMetrics.name: RSI
keyMetrics.interpretation: Neutral to bearish zone. About 7% below 50-day moving average

growthDrivers.title: Terafab AI Chip Fab Confirmed for 3/21 (Saturday) Launch
growthDrivers.description: Reuters (3/14): Musk confirmed Terafab project launch on 3/21. AI5 custom chip manufacturing facility, ending NVIDIA dependency. Low-volume production in 2026, mass production planned for 2027.

growthDrivers.title: Optimus 3 Robot Summer Production + Fremont Factory Conversion
growthDrivers.description: Teslarati (3/14): Musk confirmed Optimus 3 low-rate production starting this summer. WIRED (3/16): Model S/X production ended, Fremont factory converting to Optimus robots. 2027 mass production target.

growthDrivers.title: China Jan-Feb Sales +35% YoY, 127,728 Units
growthDrivers.description: Finbold (3/15): China-made Tesla EV sales surged 35%+ to 127,728 units in Jan-Feb. European Feb registrations also +10% YoY.

growthDrivers.title: UK Electricity Supply License, Energy Business Expansion
growthDrivers.description: Reuters (3/12): Ofgem approved Tesla Energy for electricity supply license. Texas model expanding to Europe.

growthDrivers.title: Premarket +2.47% ($400.88), Terafab Anticipation Building
growthDrivers.description: Motley Fool (3/16): TSLA up 2.47% premarket Monday. Terafab launch 5 days away. Weekly direction depends on Terafab and GTC outcomes.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: US EV market leader with 50%+ share. FSD 8.2B miles of data, Supercharger NACS standard, and vertical integration as core moats. However, BYD has overtaken Tesla globally and the gap is widening.

moats.description: FSD 8.2 billion miles of driving data. Overwhelmingly more training data than competitors.
In-house battery (4680), custom chips (Terafab AI5), software (FSD), charging infrastructure (Supercharger).
NACS is the North American EV charging standard. GM, Ford, Rivian depend on Tesla Superchargers.

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: Global EV sales leader (2.26M in 2025 vs Tesla 1.64M). 11 new models, 640-mile sedan, 5-min fast charging unveiled

competitors.name: Lucid (LCID)
competitors.detail: Premium EV sedan. Saudi investment backing

recentNews.headline: Motley Fool: 'What should scare Tesla investors isn't that BYD caught up, it's how fast they're pulling ahead'
recentNews.significance: BYD 2025 EVs: 2.26M (+28%) vs Tesla 1.64M (-8%). BYD unveiled 11 new models, 640-mile sedan, 5-min fast charging. Competition accelerating

recentNews.headline: Finbold: Gordon Johnson (GJL) warns Tesla could crash 80% to $78.88 by H2 2026
recentNews.significance: If FSD fails to deliver by H1 2026 deadline, 80% of valuation could evaporate. Extreme bear case but highlights FSD risk

recentNews.headline: CoinCentral: Terafab AI chip fab launch just days away. Musk's latest moonshot
recentNews.significance: 3/21 launch D-5. Custom AI5 chip production to reduce NVIDIA dependency. Success is the week's biggest catalyst

recentNews.headline: 200-day moving average broken to the downside. First time since September. ATH -20%+ bear market
recentNews.significance: Institutional selling accelerating. Calydon -62%, Bamco -8%. Technical bearish reversal

recentNews.headline: Syrah and Tesla extend graphite supply default deadline to June 1
recentNews.significance: Battery supply chain uncertainty persists, though both companies working to resolve

analystOpinions.notableComment: Consensus $406.84 (+4%), majority Hold. Dan Ives at $600 (bull), Gordon Johnson at $78.88 (-80%, extreme bear). FSD deadline and Terafab results will determine target direction.

risks.title: BYD Competition Accelerating, Global EV Share Reversed
risks.description: Motley Fool (3/16): BYD sold 2.26M EVs (+28%) in 2025 vs Tesla 1.64M (-8%). BYD unveiled 11 new models, 640-mile sedan, 5-min fast charging. Tesla's aging lineup risks falling further behind.

risks.title: 200-Day Moving Average Break, Bear Market Entry
risks.description: First close below 200-day MA since September. Down 20%+ from ATH. Institutional selling accelerating (Calydon -62%, Bamco -8%).

risks.title: FSD H1 2026 Deadline Risk
risks.description: Gordon Johnson (3/16): If FSD fails to achieve full autonomy by H1 2026, 80% of valuation could evaporate. Points to Musk's history of broken FSD promises.

risks.title: Extreme Valuation at P/E 362x
risks.description: TTM P/E 362x, Forward P/E 189x. Growth deceleration and intensifying competition could trigger sharp multiple compression.

risks.title: Musk Leadership Distraction + DOGE Controversy
risks.description: Simultaneously running Tesla, xAI, SpaceX, X (Twitter), DOGE. WIRED (3/16): Tesla influencers leaving the 'cult.'

buyReasons.title: Terafab AI Chip 3/21 Launch, Hardware Self-Sufficiency
buyReasons.rationale: Custom AI5 chip production ending NVIDIA dependency. Premarket +2.47% already pricing in expectations. Launch success is the near-term catalyst.

buyReasons.title: China Jan-Feb Sales +35%, Europe +10% Recovery
buyReasons.rationale: Gaining share back vs BYD in China. But still trailing in global total deliveries, so sustainability needs monitoring.

buyReasons.title: Optimus Robot + Fremont Factory Conversion
buyReasons.rationale: Model S/X ended, factory converting to Optimus production. If successful, entry into $12T+ robotics TAM. Pivot from EV to robotics.

buyReasons.title: Energy Business European Expansion, UK Power License
buyReasons.rationale: Ofgem license enables direct electricity sales in UK. Megapack + power supply diversifies revenue streams.

overallOpinion: Terafab AI chip factory launch is 5 days away (3/21). Premarket is up 2.47% to $400.88 with growing anticipation. China sales surged 35% in Jan-Feb, regaining share from BYD.

But BYD is pulling ahead fast. It sold 2.26M EVs in 2025 (+28%) vs Tesla's 1.64M (-8%), and just unveiled a 640-mile sedan with 5-minute charging. Gordon Johnson warns an 80% crash to $78.88 if FSD misses its H1 2026 deadline.

Closed at $391.20. Below both the 50-day ($420) and 200-day ($425) averages at P/E 362x. Consensus target $407 (+4%). Terafab launch outcome will determine the near-term direction. Waiting for confirmation is a reasonable approach.

investmentVerdict.summary: Terafab 3/21 launch is the near-term catalyst, but BYD competition acceleration and 200-day MA breakdown are concerns. At P/E 362x with FSD deadline risk, waiting for Terafab results before new positions is prudent.

BYD sold 2.26M EVs (+28%) in 2025, overtaking Tesla's 1.64M (-8%). They just unveiled 11 new models including a 640-mile sedan and 5-minute fast charging. Tesla ended Model S/X production, pivoting Fremont to Optimus robots.

witty.hook: BYD unveils a 640-mile sedan. Tesla shuts down S/X to build robots
witty.text:
$TSLA (3/16) $391.20 (-0.96%)

BYD brought 11 new models, a 640-mile sedan, and 5-minute charging. Tesla's response? End Model S/X and build robots at Fremont. Different galaxies at this point.

China is up 35% and Saturday brings Terafab. Johnson says $78.88, Ives says $600. Same stock, wildly different math.

P/E 362x below the 200-day. If Terafab delivers, Musk's pivot looks visionary. If not, BYD's headline writes itself.

empathy.hook: Tesla shareholders, the BYD news stings but Saturday matters
empathy.text:
$TSLA (3/16) $391.20 (-0.96%)

BYD overtaking with 2.26M units and a 640-mile sedan is hard to digest. The aging lineup losing ground is real.

But China sales are up 35%, premarket +2.47%. Terafab AI chip launch on Saturday is confirmed. The pivot to AI, robotics, and energy is Tesla's real bet.

P/E 362x, consensus $407 (+4%). If you're anxious, waiting for Saturday's outcome is perfectly reasonable. The opportunity is still there.
